req.title: Lab order routing
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct routing scenarios: (1) preferred lab is available → route to preferred lab (positive/EP); (2) preferred lab is unavailable → fallback to default lab (negative/NEG/DT); (3) boundary state where a lab transitions from available to unavailable mid-order cycle (STC). A fourth BVA case covers a patient with exactly one lab preference (the minimum non-zero preference count). Together these provide EP, NEG, DT, STC, and BVA coverage of R-HLT-018.

tests.name: test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available
preconditions: Authenticated clinician session token available via <clinician_auth_fixture>
Patient record exists with preferred lab set to 'lab-preferred-001'
Lab 'lab-preferred-001' is marked active/available in the system
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject referencing patient, code for CBC, and performer hint omitted (system should resolve preferred lab)
Capture the HTTP response and the created ServiceRequest resource
trace: R-HLT-018: order routed to preferred lab when available
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available(
    clinician_auth_fixture,
    patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture,
    ensure_lab_available_fixture,
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture
    patient_id = patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture["patient_id"]
    preferred_lab = patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture["preferred_lab_ref"]
    ensure_lab_available_fixture(preferred_lab, available=True)

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    resource = resp.json()
    assert resource.get("status") == "active"
    assert resource.get("id") is not None
    performers = resource.get("performer", [])
    assert any(preferred_lab in p.get("reference", "") for p in performers), \
        f"Expected performer {preferred_lab}, got {performers}"

tests.name: test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Authenticated clinician session token available via <clinician_auth_fixture>
Patient record exists with preferred lab 'lab-preferred-001'
Lab 'lab-preferred-001' is marked inactive/unavailable in the system
A default lab 'lab-default-001' is configured as system fallback
actions: Mark preferred lab as unavailable via <ensure_lab_available_fixture>
POST /ServiceRequest with the same patient and CBC code
trace: R-HLT-018: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable(
    clinician_auth_fixture,
    patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture,
    ensure_lab_available_fixture,
    default_lab_ref_fixture,
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture
    patient_id = patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture["patient_id"]
    preferred_lab = patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture["preferred_lab_ref"]
    default_lab = default_lab_ref_fixture
    ensure_lab_available_fixture(preferred_lab, available=False)

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    resource = resp.json()
    assert resource.get("status") == "active"
    performers = resource.get("performer", [])
    refs = [p.get("reference", "") for p in performers]
    assert any(default_lab in r for r in refs), f"Expected fallback {default_lab}, got {refs}"
    assert not any(preferred_lab in r for r in refs), f"Preferred lab should not appear: {refs}"

tests.name: test_order_status_transitions_from_draft_to_active_on_submission
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab available
A draft ServiceRequest exists for the patient (status=draft)
actions: PUT /ServiceRequest/{id} updating status from 'draft' to 'active' to simulate clinician finalising the order
expected: ServiceRequest.performer is populated (routing applied on activation)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_status_transitions_from_draft_to_active_on_submission(
    clinician_auth_fixture,
    draft_service_request_fixture,
    patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture,
    ensure_lab_available_fixture,
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture
    preferred_lab = patient_with_preferred_lab_fixture["preferred_lab_ref"]
    ensure_lab_available_fixture(preferred_lab, available=True)
    sr_id = draft_service_request_fixture["id"]
    draft_resource = draft_service_request_fixture.copy()
    draft_resource["status"] = "active"

    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    put_resp = requests.put(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", json=draft_resource, headers=headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    resource = get_resp.json()
    assert resource.get("status") == "active", f"Expected active, got {resource.get('status')}"
    assert len(resource.get("performer", [])) >= 1, "Performer must be set after activation"

tests.name: test_order_routing_with_exactly_one_lab_preference
preconditions: Patient record exists with exactly ONE preferred lab entry (minimum valid preference list)
That single preferred lab is available
actions: POST /ServiceRequest for the patient with exactly one lab preference
expected: The single performer reference matches the one configured preferred lab
No duplicate or extra performers appended
trace: R-HLT-018: order routed to preferred lab (BVA: exactly one preference, lower boundary of non-empty preference set)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_routing_with_exactly_one_lab_preference(
    clinician_auth_fixture,
    patient_with_single_lab_preference_fixture,
    ensure_lab_available_fixture,
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture
    patient_id = patient_with_single_lab_preference_fixture["patient_id"]
    sole_lab = patient_with_single_lab_preference_fixture["preferred_lab_ref"]
    ensure_lab_available_fixture(sole_lab, available=True)

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    resource = resp.json()
    performers = resource.get("performer", [])
    assert len(performers) == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 performer, got {len(performers)}"
    assert sole_lab in performers[0].get("reference", ""), \
        f"Expected {sole_lab} as sole performer, got {performers[0]}"

tests.name: test_order_routing_decision_table_no_preferred_lab_configured
preconditions: Patient record exists with NO preferred lab configured
Default lab 'lab-default-001' is configured as system fallback
actions: POST /ServiceRequest for a patient who has no preferred lab
trace: R-HLT-018: DT rule — no preference configured → always route to default lab
R-HLT-018: error pathway: fallback to default lab
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_order_routing_decision_table_no_preferred_lab_configured(
    clinician_auth_fixture,
    patient_without_lab_preference_fixture,
    default_lab_ref_fixture,
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture
    patient_id = patient_without_lab_preference_fixture["patient_id"]
    default_lab = default_lab_ref_fixture

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    resource = resp.json()
    assert resource.get("status") == "active"
    performers = resource.get("performer", [])
    refs = [p.get("reference", "") for p in performers]
    assert any(default_lab in r for r in refs), \
        f"Expected default lab {default_lab} as performer, got {refs}"
